mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the Unemployment Rate over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production over 6 months signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decline in Housing Starts over 3 months signals a decline in demand, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Unemployment Rate over 3 months signals a loosening labor market, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-unemployment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate and a 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index over 6 months signal a tightening monetary cycle and rising inflation expectations, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1% increase and CPIAUCSL > 2% increase in 6 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
